BALABAC, GUIMARAS (photos), HUNDRED ISLS (photos), LUZON: Pangasinan (photos), PALAWAN.
species.notes: Certainly an introduced weed in the Philippines. Throughout the archipelago in open waste places at low and medium elevation.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 529 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 315; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 23; Liu & Lo in Huang (ed.), Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 3 (1993) 727.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, China, India, Indian Ocean, Laos, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand.
species.notes: Cultivated or naturalized throughout the tropics. In the Philippines apparently not planted. In clearings, on rice paddy banks, and in open, low, usually wet places in and near settlements throughout the archipelago. Jute.

species.citation: Lam. , Encycl. (Lamarck) 2 (1786) 104 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 23.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 529 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 315; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 23; Liu & Lo in Huang (ed.), Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 3 (1993) 727; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 438.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam, W Asia. HOMONHON, LUZON: Rizal (photos).
species.notes: Pantropic but a native of the Old World (said to be a native of India). Throughout the Philippines in and near settlements, on rice paddy banks, fallow paddies, etc. Occasionally cultivated as a vegetable.
